user_message: You are conducting a release retrospective for EntireContext v0.4.0 Feed the Loop. This is a time-travel searchable agent memory system (Python 3.12+, uv, SQLite). Do NOT make any code changes. <task> Analyze the v0.4.0 release and produce a structured retrospective. Read the codebase as needed to ground your findings. Release facts: - 7 PRs merged (#82, #83/#88, #84/#89, #85/#87, #86/#90, #91, #92) - 19 files changed, 2674 insertions, 66 deletions - 1530 tests total (up from ~1463 at v0.3.0, so +67 tests) - All delivered in a single day (2026-04-17) Features: - F1: Outcome recency decay in quality score (exponential decay, half_life_days=30, min_volume=2; decay only in ranking path) - F2: Outcome → extraction confidence penalty (aggregated-across-files ratio, hardcoded 0.5 threshold, hard lookback window) - F3: Ranking weight config [decisions.ranking]; additive-only score_breakdown key contract - F4: UserPromptSubmit async decision surfacing with triple-layer in-memory redaction, O_EXCL+0600 tmp files, defense-in-depth worker re-redaction - E2E: Integration test wiring all 4 features with negative assertions Deferred debt still pending (deferred from v0.2.0 retro, then again from v0.3.0): - D.4: confirm_candidate atomicity (commit-free helper chain) - D.5: SQLite autocommit migration - D.6: Review-bot noise reduction Answer these 6 questions with concrete evidence from the codebase where possible: 1. Process: All 4 features + E2E + release in one day — is this velocity sustainable? What risks does single-day concentration introduce? 2. Design quality: Are there any design decisions that seem under-justified or might need revisiting? 3. Deferred debt: The 3 deferred items from v0.2.0 retro were supposed to be v0.3.0 scope, then deferred again. Is this deferral pattern concerning? 4. Test coverage: +67 tests for 2674 new lines. Is this adequate? Any gaps? 5. Security: F4 has a complex security model (triple redaction, O_EXCL, 0600). Is this over-engineered or appropriate? 6. v0.5.0 focus: What should the next release prioritize? </task> <structured_output_contract> Return a structured retrospective with these exact sections: ## What Went Well ## What Could Improve ## Risks and Concerns ## Answer to Each Question (1-6, with evidence citations where possible) ## Recommendations for v0.5.0 Keep each section concise. Cite specific files and line numbers when making empirical claims. Label hypotheses clearly if you cannot verify them from source. </structured_output_contract> <grounding_rules> - Read relevant source files before making claims about implementation details - Cite file:line for specific code observations - Label hypothesis for any claim you cannot verify directly from source - Do not invent implementation details not present in the code </grounding_rules> <default_follow_through_policy> Read the codebase as needed. Do not ask clarifying questions. Produce the retrospective document directly. </default_follow_through_policy>
assistant_summary: v0.4.0 릴리스 회고를 코드 기준으로 정리하겠습니다. 먼저 결정/교훈 메모와 릴리스 관련 소스·테스트를 읽어서, 각 질문에 필요한 근거를 파일/라인 단위로 모으겠습니다.
